Synthesize structural facts across multiple codebooks

Source: R/06_manuscript_learning.R
dot-synthesize_cross_study_patterns.Rd

Produces a domain-neutral, evidence-based summary of the prior codebooks' actual contents (theme names, hierarchy depth, coding style benchmarks).

Usage

.synthesize_cross_study_patterns(hierarchies, studies, benchmarks)

Arguments

hierarchies

Named list of codebook hierarchy data

studies

PreviousStudies object

benchmarks

Computed coding benchmarks (or NULL)

Value

Character string describing structural facts (no opinions)

Details

Design notes

An earlier version of this function (pre-1.0.0) injected hardcoded medication/health-research opinions into the AI's learning context: a regex list that matched theme names to predefined "recurring categories" (side effects, treatment efficacy, dosage timing, etc.) and an unconditional narrative-arc claim ("themes were organized to tell a coherent story: starting with direct treatment effects, moving to side effects and complications, then broader implications...") that fired whenever any prior codebook had theme descriptions. Both biased the AI toward medication-research framings regardless of the user's actual research domain. They have been removed.

What this function does now: list the actual top-level theme names from each prior codebook so the AI can see what was studied without being told what the patterns "are". The numerical benchmarks (segment length, codes per entry, discarded-code percentage) are kept because they're domain-independent calibration data.

On this page
